subject: intro: reed x lucas re: the slate deal
body:
Hi loyd,

Looking to get a 30-min intro on the books between Reed and Lucas next week re: the slate deal. Zoom is fine. Can you share Reed's avails?

Thanks, Alice
notes: Canonical new-meeting request: explicit duration, attendees, format, and an avails ask.

subject: Re: intro: reed x lucas re: the slate deal
thread_so_far: Meeting was confirmed for Tue, May 26 at 2pm PT — Reed x Lucas re: the slate deal. Alice is Lucas's desk.
body:
Hi loyd,

Lucas got pulled into a screening — can we push this to later in the week? Thu or Fri would work better.

Thanks, Alice
notes: Genuine reschedule: a confirmed meeting exists in thread_so_far and the sender asks to 'push' it. Contrast E16, where no meeting is booked.

subject: Re: signing prep call
thread_so_far: Meeting was confirmed for Wed, May 27 at 10am PT — Reed x Ben re: the signing prep.
body:
loyd,

Gonna have to cancel tomorrow's prep call. Ben's flying out for a label meeting that just came through. Will circle back next week.

Best, Ben's desk
notes: Explicit cancel of a confirmed meeting. 'Will circle back next week' is a soft future intent, not a reschedule request — no new time proposed.

body: what do i have on thursday?
notes: Canonical agenda query, iMessage channel. Lowercase, no punctuation — channel style differs sharply from email.

body: block monday 2-4 for the screening review pls
notes: Canonical calendar block, iMessage channel. Explicit time range on the user's own calendar.

subject: Today: indie studio closes $300M co-fi; three IP greenlights at major studio
body:
Today's top stories:

1. Indie studio closes $300M co-fi deal with European group — biggest indie close of the year
2. Major studio greenlights three new IP adaptations for 2027 slate
3. Festival circuit announces summer routing for top-billed acts
4. Music division reshuffle at major label

[unsubscribe]
notes: Industry newsletter. Loyd not on To/Cc — reached the agent via indirect path. Dense industry shorthand (co-fi, slate, routing) is decoy vocabulary, not intent.

subject: synced with hannah yesterday
body:
Hey Reed,

Hannah mentioned you wanted to connect on the slate co-fi. Let me know what makes sense from your end.

Theo
notes: Person-to-person message addressed to Reed, Loyd only cc'd. Gestures at a future connect but makes no scheduling ask of the desk and proposes no time. Calibration pair with E13 (same sender, more concrete).

subject: Re: reed/owen — distribution chat
thread_so_far: Loyd proposed three slots for next Tuesday for a Reed x Owen meeting re: distribution. Owen hasn't picked yet.
body: Sounds good — happy to chat.
notes: Intent is unambiguously schedule (active new-meeting negotiation in thread). Body carries no slot selection — a downstream _parse_fields/slot challenge, not an intent ambiguity.

subject: that lunch we kept missing
body:
reed — been meaning to circle back on lunch. let me know when you're around, no rush. would love to catch up on the talent situation.

lucas
notes: Soft social scheduling intent: wants to set up a lunch but deliberately vague ('no rush', 'when you're around'). Loyd on To line. Leans schedule vs E07, where Loyd is only cc'd and there is no calendar-specific ask.

subject: did reed see the deck?
body:
Hey,

Just wanted to confirm Reed saw the project deck we sent Monday for the Q4 release. Happy to jump on a quick call if he has notes.

Thanks, Caleb
notes: Primary ask is a status question about a deck, not the calendar. 'Happy to jump on a call if he has notes' is a conditional offer, not a booking request. 'did reed see' is a decoy for query_agenda, which covers the user's own calendar only.

subject: Re: festival routing call tomorrow
thread_so_far: Meeting was set for tomorrow at 3pm PT — Reed x Kai re: the festival routing. Both had confirmed.
body: Quick question — is this still on?
notes: Third party asking whether a confirmed meeting still stands. query_agenda is defined as the user querying their OWN calendar, so this fits poorly; it is the least-wrong of the six because the agent should answer, not act. Argues for a meeting_query label.

subject: Re: Lunch re: signing
body: next week?
notes: Two-word reply with no thread_so_far. 'Re: Lunch' implies an existing thread but not necessarily a booked meeting — schedule vs reschedule cannot be resolved from the message alone. A thin-context case.

subject: next steps on the co-fi deal
body:
reed — wanted to circle back on the co-fi deal we kicked around last quarter. would be great to catch up in person when you're in NY next month for the upfronts. let me know if you have a window.

theo
notes: Calibration pair with E07 (same sender). More concrete here: 'let me know if you have a window' is a calendar-specific ask, tipping it toward schedule despite being addressed to Reed with Loyd only cc'd.

subject: Re: reed/caleb avails — distribution
thread_so_far: Loyd proposed times for a Reed x Caleb meeting re: distribution. No times confirmed yet.
body: Let me check with my team and get back to you tomorrow — we're juggling a few release windows.
notes: Deferral reply inside an active new-meeting negotiation. Intent is clearly schedule; no slot selected (downstream parse challenge, like E08).

subject: Re: reed/alice/ben — slate co-fi
thread_so_far: Meeting is being set between Reed, Alice and Ben re: the slate co-fi. Currently in slot negotiation.
body:
Hi loyd, looping in Hannah from our side as well — she'll be the lead on this and should be on the meeting.

Thanks, Alice
notes: Adds an attendee to a meeting still in slot negotiation. schedule is defensible because the meeting is not yet booked, but the action itself (attendee change) has no dedicated label. Contrast E17, where the meeting is already confirmed.

subject: Re: reed/maya — tour routing
thread_so_far: Loyd proposed three slots in the May 12–18 range for a Reed x Maya tour-routing meeting. Maya hasn't picked yet.
body: Hey, none of those work — Maya's on the European leg through end of month. Can you share times for the first week of June instead?
notes: Reschedule decoy: 'none of those work, share new times' looks like a move, but no meeting is booked (Maya hasn't picked) — still new-meeting negotiation. Reschedule requires an existing confirmed meeting (see E02).

subject: Re: reed/alice/ben tuesday — signing
thread_so_far: Meeting set for next Tuesday — Reed x Alice x Ben re: the signing. Both Alice and Ben are attending.
body: actually ben isn't going to make this one — he's on a label call. just keep it as me and alice.
notes: Removes an attendee from a CONFIRMED meeting; time unchanged, meeting not cancelled. No clean label: reschedule (chosen) routes to 'modify existing meeting' but implies a time change; none is also defensible. Strongest case for a modify_attendees label.

subject: Re: tomorrow's chairman call
body: Can you send a heads up to the team that I'll be 10 min late tomorrow? Coming off another call. Don't move anything, just let them know.
notes: Reschedule decoy with an explicit negation: '10 min late' suggests a time change but 'Don't move anything' rules it out. It is a notify-attendees request — no calendar write — so none is least-wrong. Argues for a notify_attendees label.

subject: Re: reed/lucas — brand integration
thread_so_far: Loyd has proposed slots for a Reed x Lucas meeting re: the brand integration. Multiple back-and-forth.
body: Just confirming — is the meeting Zoom or in person? Lucas needs to know for prep.
notes: Question about a meeting's format, not about calendar contents. query_agenda (own-calendar) fits poorly but is least-wrong since the agent should answer. Pairs with E11 — both are information requests about an existing meeting that the label space cannot express.

subject: Re: lock with reed — wed 3pm
body: Locked in. See you Wednesday.
notes: Slot acceptance — the closing 'lock' step of a new-meeting negotiation (subject names the slot). Folds into schedule; a pure-acknowledgment reading (none) is weaker because the agent must mark the meeting confirmed.

subject: Re: reed/caleb/ben — deal review
thread_so_far: Meeting was proposed for next Tuesday at 2pm PT — Reed x Caleb x Ben re: the deal review. Multiple attendees on thread.
body: Tuesday 2pm doesn't work for me but let me find someone else from our side to take it. Will let you know who.
notes: Meeting only proposed, not booked, so 'doesn't work' is not a reschedule — still negotiation (schedule). Adds an attendee-substitution wrinkle with deferred info ('will let you know who').

subject: Reminder
body: remind me to send the executed deal to alice on friday
notes: A task reminder, not a meeting and not a time block. block_calendar is least-wrong as the only self-directed calendar-write label, but it implies holding a time range, which this is not. Argues for a set_reminder / task label.

subject: You have a document to sign
body:
Hi Reed Martin,

Ben Carter has requested your signature on a document: 'Talent Representation Agreement — 2026'.

[Review Document]

Thank you, E-Sign Service
notes: Automated transactional notification. Loyd not on To/Cc — indirect path. No scheduling intent.

subject: Festival submission received — Project SR
body:
Hi Reed,

We've received your submission for Project SR (Director: M. Cleves). Programming committee meets in the next 4–6 weeks. We'll reach out with a decision.

Thanks, Festival Programming
notes: Automated confirmation. 'Committee meets in the next 4–6 weeks' is meeting vocabulary about a third party's calendar — a decoy for schedule. Loyd not on To/Cc — indirect path.

subject: loved the slate pitch
body:
reed — wanted to say great work on the slate pitch last night. team is buzzing about the second-window project. talk soon.

nora
notes: Social/congratulatory note. 'talk soon' is a sign-off pleasantry, a decoy for schedule. Loyd not on To/Cc — indirect path.
